new_tenders.solicitation_title: Calibration of Radiation Measuring Equipment
new_tenders.gsin_description: Calibration of Radiation Measuring Equipment The Department of National Defence has a requirement for the Calibration (Alpha, Beta, Gamma and Neutron) of Radiation Measuring Equipment to support the Directorate of Combat Support Equipment Management 5 (DCSEM 5). The work will involve receiving, calibrating, certifying to the appropriate standards and returning calibrated systems; as well as working collaboratively with OEM and other contractors. This procurement is subject to the Controlled Goods Program. The Defence production Act defines Canadian Controlled Goods as certain goods listed in Canada’s Export Control List, a regulation made pursuant to the Export and Import Permits Act (EIPA). The Crown retains the rights to negotiate with suppliers on any procurement. Documents may be submitted in either official language of Canada.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Rental of temporary sanitation facilities
new_tenders.gsin_description: Canada is seeking offers from Offerors to provide temporary sanitation facilities to support personnel logging in temporary shelters. The Canadian Forces School of Leadership and Recruits requires sixteen (16) portable toilets, two (2) ten (10) unit sanitation trailers, and eight (8) portable handwashing stations to Department of National Defence.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Digitial Video Borescope Systems
new_tenders.gsin_description: Department of Naitonal Defence has a requirement for portable, lightweight, handheld video borescope system for use in training and operations. The system will be used to provide discrete penetration and visual recognition within inaccessible environments.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Call for Proposals (CFP) Procurement and Operationalization of…
new_tenders.gsin_description: Notice of Proposed Procurement (NPP) CALL FOR PROPOSALS (CFP) W7714-26885 REQUIREMENT : Public Works and Government Services Canada (PWGSC) is issuing a Call for Proposals (CFP) for the pilot Procurement and Operationalization of Innovation and New Technology (POINT) initiative under the Innovation for Defence Excellence and Security (IDEaS) Program, supported by the Bureau of Research, Engineering and Advanced Leadership in Innovation and Science (BOREALIS). *For additional general information on the IDEaS Program, visit: https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/programs/defence-i… _____________ The solicitation for this requirement is seeking offers for solutions to the Challenge titled “Sentinel Shield: Wide-area Detection for Early Warning Against Uncrewed Aerial Systems”. Solutions must have successfully completed Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 5 and be ready to advance to TRL 6 or higher in order to be considered. This procurement is to be carried out in two phases: • Phase 1- Build & Test (TRL 6-9) • Phase 2- Acquisition Steps to apply: Step 1: Read the Challenge Details Step 2: Read the solicitation document: See bidding details section Step 3: Propose your solution here : https://defence-innovation-portal.my.site.com/ _____________ SOLICATION PERIOD : From 2025-03-26 to 2025-06-24 2:00pm EDT Please refer to the solicitation documents for information related to offer preparation and submission instructions, evaluation procedures, basis of selection and other pertinent information. CANADIAN SUPPLIERS: This requirement is solely limited to Canadian Suppliers TESTING DEPARTMENTS: Department of National Defence (DND) / The Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) FUNDING MECHANISM: Contract ESTIMATED MAXIUMUM BUDGET The estimated maximum total combined budget available for all resulting contracts under Phase 1 - Build & Test is $10,000,000.00 (applicable taxes, travel, living and shipping excluded). The estimated maximum budget available for each individual Phase 1 - Build & Test contract is $1,000,000.00 (applicable taxes, travel, living and shipping excluded). The estimated maximum budget available for Phase 2- Acquisition is $20,000,000.00 (applicable taxes, travel, living and shipping excluded) for a sufficient number of units to cover up to 27 sites of at least 10 km radius each. This disclosure is made in good faith and does not commit Canada to contract for the total approximate budget. Should fiscal funding no longer be available, Offerors will be notified directly. The Contract Award Process will continue and proposals under contract negotiations will take priority when funding is available. Multiple contracts could result from this requirement. This estimated budget is not a contract guarantee. Conditionally qualified proposals that are matched and selected for contract award may not necessarily receive the total maximum contract value. Individual contract values will be negotiated during the Contract Award Process. QUESTIONS DURING SOLICITATION PERIOD: Frequently asked questions, as well as on-going Offeror questions will be answered and may be published in an amendment to the Notice Document. PERIOD FOR QUESTIONS All enquiries must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Authority at: TPSGC.PAIDEES-APIDEAS.PWGSC@tpsgc-pwgsc.gc.ca no later than seven business days before the Notice closing date. Enquiries received after that time may not be answered. ____________ Challenge Details: See Annex C - Challenge Statement attachment of the Call for Proposals (CFP) for further details.
new_tenders.client: Department of Public Works and Government Services (PSPC)
new_tenders.contact_name: PWGSC IDEaS Team / L'équipe IDEeS de TPSGC

new_tenders.solicitation_title: EC015-261761 - RFSO - Floating Plant Dredging - NB Gulf Region
new_tenders.gsin_description: Public Services and Procurement Canada (PSPC), on behalf of the Department of Fisheries and Oceans has a requirement for a Regional Individual Standing Offer (RISO) that includes, but is not limited to carrying out floating plant dredging at various public wharves and entrance channels (including gullies) along the east coast of the province of New Brunswick This RISO will be from April 1, 2026 or issuance of Standing Offer to March 31st, 2028 inclusive The estimated cost for this opportunity is within the following cost category: between $1,000,001 to $5,000,000 (HST included) This solicitation of offers is open only to Canadian suppliers Bidders may request a debriefing on the results of the bid solicitation. Bidders should make the request to the Contracting Authority within 15 working days from receipt of notification that their bid was unsuccessful. Reference the solicitation for further details. Reference the solicitation for further details.
new_tenders.client: Department of Public Works and Government Services (PSPC)

new_tenders.solicitation_title: W0113-26CS14 Vehicle Maintenance and Repairs – MSVS MILCOTS
new_tenders.gsin_description: AMENDMENT TO CLOSING TIME: Please disregard the Ariba Discovery posting response deadline closing time and CanadaBuys posting closing time. All required supporting documentation and bid submission must be submitted by 2026-04-21 at 14:00 EDT. All bids submitted after 14:00 EDT will result in the bid being declared non-responsive. Canada is seeking offers from Offerors to provide maintenance and repairs of Medium Support Vehicle System (MSVS) Militarized Commercial off the Shelf (MILCOTs). The requirement is “as and when required” for the provision of all labour, parts, and equipment necessary for the diagnostic, repair, and refurbishment services of the MSVS MILCOT – Navistar Internationals 7400 (SFA 6x6) and/or International WorkStar from 2009 to Department of National Defence (DND), Canadian Forces Base (CFB) Borden, Technical Services. The requirement includes two (2) streams (Stream 1 All Suppliers and Stream 2 Procurement Strategy for Indigenous Business). Streams will be evaluated separately, with the lowest priced offer for each stream issued a Standing Offer.
new_tenders.client: Department of Public Works and Government Services (PSPC)

new_tenders.solicitation_title: FOX-A and FOX-1 Remediation Construction
new_tenders.gsin_description: Please note that late submissions will not be considered or accepted. A Letter of Interest is a public notice indicating to the supplier community that a department or agency intends to solicit proposals for a contract for goods, services or construction, thereby allowing proponents to signal their interest in participating in a site visit or in submitting a proposal. Public Services and Procurement Canada (PSPC), on behalf of Crown Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Canada (CIRNAC) is issuing a notice of optional pre-tender site visit and intended procurement for the remediation of the FOX-A and FOX-1 Sites on Bray Island and Rowley Island, Nunavut.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: NPP - Senior - Special Advisor - Digital Modernization
new_tenders.gsin_description: THS - Senior - Special Advisor Data Science, AI and Information Solutions (DSDAI) requires the temporary services of a Strategic Advisor to provide support to the team during a period of increased workload. The resource will assist in supporting the understanding, adoption, and use of modern technologies such as Microsoft Dynamics 365, Power Platform, and other cloud-based solutions.The resource will provide support to business units in the design, development, and improvement of applications and workflows, while assisting teams in strengthening their internal capacity to manage and maintain their digital solutions. The services will consist of providing advice, guidance, and operational support related to ongoing IM/IT digital modernization activities.
new_tenders.client: Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development (GAC)
